data.title: India cabinet approves new regional air connectivity scheme for $3 billion
data.description:
NEW DELHI, March 25 (Reuters) - India's cabinet approved on Wednesday a scheme to expand regional air connectivity within the country at a cost of 288.40 billion rupees ($3.06 billion), Information Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw told reporters.

($1 = 94.1880 Indian rupees)

(Reporting by Sakshi Dayal, writing by Tanvi Mehta; Editing by YP Rajesh)

German army eyes AI tools to expedite wartime decision-making
data.description:
BERLIN, March 25 (Reuters) - The German army is working to accelerate wartime decision-making through artificial intelligence tools capable of analysing battlefield data more rapidly than humans, drawing lessons from Ukrainian and other forces, its commander told Reuters.

Lieutenant General Christian Freuding, who became army chief last October after years of overseeing Germany's arms supplies to Kyiv, shared his insights from visits to Ukrainian command posts where drones and modern sensors have drastically increased the volume of battlefield data.  

"The Ukrainians exploit data which they have collected over four years of war. Based on this data, the AI can deduce how the enemy has acted in similar situations in the past - and recommend countermeasures," he said.

He noted that tasks now requiring hundreds of personnel and days to complete could be sped up significantly through AI, adding that conventional methods alone would never be enough to "break the adversary's decision-making cycle".

Freuding suggested utilising data from Ukraine and from German military exercises when training analytical tools, ensuring alignment with Germany's operational principles.

Addressing ethical concerns, he emphasised that AI would serve only as an advisory tool to facilitate human decision-making. 

"The task of taking analytical and balanced decisions will always remain with the human, with the soldier," he said, adding that while a specific AI product is yet to be selected, the technology's deployment is a priority.     

Freuding underscored the importance of aligning Germany's AI systems with NATO's evolving standards. He did not rule out a European-developed system, but said American solutions might offer practical advantages due to their advanced deployment.

"Personally, I think it's important that we get something up and running quickly. Of course, issues like data sovereignty and security need to be taken into account," he added. 

The U.S. army is fielding the AI tool Maven, made by the Silicon Valley company Palantir, to process battlefield data, including imagery and video, to improve situational awareness and speed up decision-making.

(Reporting by Sabine Siebold, editing by Andrei Khalip)

By Sabine Siebold

data.title: Kuaishou Continues Earnings Growth Amid AI Push
data.description:
By Jason Chau


Kuaishou Technology, China's second-largest short-video platform, delivered broadly in-line quarterly and annual results, supported by revenue growth in its core advertising and e-commerce businesses as its continued its artificial-intelligence push.

Fourth-quarter net profit jumped 32% from a year earlier to 5.23 billion yuan, equivalent to $758.8 million. Adjusting for share-based compensation expenses and net fair-value changes on investments, profit rose 16% during the period.

Quarterly revenue also increased, climbing 12% to 39.57 billion yuan, the Beijing-based company said Wednesday.

Analysts had expected net profit of 5.38 billion yuan on revenue of 38.83 billion yuan, according to an LSEG consensus estimate.

The company attributed the increase in revenue to growth in its online marketing services, e-commerce segment and its AI business.

The results could help revive sentiment on the stock, which has lost momentum in recent months amid heightened market volatility. In 2025, Kuaishou's Hong Kong-listed shares surged nearly 55%, driven by strong investor optimism around the potential of its video-generation model, Kling AI, and the broader Chinese technology sector.

The company, known as the second-largest short video platform in China behind TikTok's sister app, Douyin, has continued to grow its daily active users, supported by a user base concentrated in lower-tier cities and rural areas. Despite intense competition from rivals like ByteDance, its average daily active users rose to 410.2 million last year from 399.4 million in 2024.

Its top and bottom lines for the full year also largely met market expectations, with net profit rising 21% to 18.62 billion yuan and revenue increasing 13% to 142.78 billion yuan. Adjusted net profit was 17% higher.

Overall, analysts have been positive on Kuaishou's outlook. The company's large user base, diversified revenue streams and prudent financial management should continue to support earnings, said CreditSights analysts.

S&P analysts said they expect its core e-commerce business, which contributes more than 40% of the company's revenue and over half of its profit, to remain a key growth driver this year.

"Kuaishou's greater proportion of rural-area users cushions it from the fierce competition surrounding instant deliveries in higher-tier cities," they wrote in a note.

Investors, meanwhile, are monitoring the progress in its AI technology. The company, which released its latest Kling 3.0 model in early February, said its AI tools now serve more than 60 million creators globally, with use cases across movies, animation and advertising.

Kling AI's revenue exceeded 340 million yuan in the fourth quarter, Kuaishou said.

While direct monetization of Kling remains limited, Kuaishou's AI capabilities are expected to support earnings growth in the coming years by reducing production and advertising costs and accelerating content creation, S&P analysts said.


Write to Jason Chau at jason.chau@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-25-26 0643ET

data.title: Fifteen points to ponder
data.description:
March 25 -

What matters in U.S. and global markets today

By Mike Dolan, Editor-At-Large, Finance and Markets

There were some glimmers of hope in the energy markets on Wednesday as oil prices slipped back and stocks caught a break after Iran indicated to the UN that it would allow some ships from non-combatant countries to pass through the Strait of Hormuz.

Meantime, Washington continued to talk up the chances of negotiation, with reports circulating of a 15-point plan involving a month-long ceasefire. Iran continues to deny any talks are underway, however, and missiles continue to fly around the region.

I'll get into that and more below.

But first, check out my latest column on how Gulf states' shaken faith in U.S. military protection could ripple through the petrodollar system.

And catch today's episode of the Morning Bid podcast, where I discuss oil's latest moves and what recent business surveys tell us about how the real economy is absorbing the energy shock.

Subscribe to hear Reuters journalists discuss the biggest news in markets and finance seven days a week.

FIFTEEN POINTS TO PONDER

Amid all the conflicting Iran war headlines coming out, traders appear to be choosing to look on the bright side. Brent crude eased to as low as $98 per barrel on Wednesday, while U.S. crude hovered around $88.

Asian equities closed comfortably up, meantime, with South Korea's KOSPI gaining around 1.5% and Japan's Nikkei nearly 3%. European shares rose by more than 1% after the open, while U.S. futures were up before the bell.

And in another sign of the buoyed sentiment, gold - which has behaved more like a risk asset lately - was up by nearly 2% on Wednesday morning as it benefited from a softer dollar and easing concerns about higher interest rates.

Nevertheless, it's clear the war has caused a significant hit to economic confidence. Tuesday saw the first sweep of business surveys from March, which showed stalling private sector growth in the eurozone, rising U.S. inflation fears and more.

Overall, uncertainty is the order of the day as it remains unclear who exactly President Trump is negotiating with and whether a swift alleviation of energy disruption can really be secured. A spokesperson for the Iranian Armed Forces accused the U.S. of negotiating with itself.

Elsewhere, private credit jitters continued as Ares joined Apollo and other asset managers to become the latest to halt redemptions after a wave of investors sought to exit certain funds.

And in technology news, South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix is reportedly planning to list in the U.S. in the second half of 2026, while Elon Musk's SpaceX could file for an IPO as soon as this week or next week, according to The Information.

Chart of the day

According to a Reuters/Ipsos poll, only 29% of the country approves of President Trump's economic stewardship, the lowest rating in either of Trump's terms and lower than any economic approval rating of his predecessor, Democrat Joe Biden.

Today's events to watch

* U.S. February import prices (4:30 PM EDT), Q4 current account (4:30 PM EDT)

* U.S. 5-year and 7-year note auctions

* Fed's Stephen Miran speaks

* ECB President Christine Lagarde speaks

Want to receive the Morning Bid in your inbox every weekday morning? Sign up for the newsletter here. You can find ROI on the Reuters website, and you can follow us on LinkedIn and X.

Opinions expressed are those of the author. They do not reflect the views of Reuters News, which, under the Trust Principles, is committed to integrity, independence, and freedom from bias.

(By Mike Dolan)

By Mike Dolan

Azimut: AHE renews partnership with Ferrari
data.description:
(Alliance News) - Azimut Holding Spa announced Wednesday the renewal of the strategic partnership between Azimut Automobile Heritage Enhancement and Ferrari Spa.

"The synergy generated between the two entities finds a perfect synthesis in the AHE portfolio, which is currently the only player in the world to boast three Ferrari 499P test cars in its portfolio, the hypercar that has triumphed at Le Mans for three consecutive years," the statement read.

"The possession of these iconic cars within the fund transforms and enhances current sporting excellence into a historical and financial asset of inestimable future value," Azimut continued.

The AHE fund manages assets exceeding EUR280 million, with a return of over 23% since its inception. Since its launch in May 2023, AHE has built a portfolio of 59 cars, 15 of which have already been placed on the market.

As a Ferrari Hypercar Team Partner, AHE will accompany the Ferrari 499Ps in the FIA World Endurance Championship and the Safety Car of the Ferrari Challenge Trofeo Pirelli, the Prancing Horse's one-make championship, with the "Azimut-AHE" logo in a new graphic design. In addition to this presence, the partnership will continue to offer the fund's investors exclusive "on track" experiences and the opportunity to access events organized at the prestigious Maranello headquarters.

"Thanks to Azimut Investments' license for the financial management of luxury cars, the fund operates through a structured process that oversees every stage, from purchase to restoration and preservation, offering High and Ultra High Net Worth investors an alternative asset class capable of outperforming other luxury real assets such as art or high-end watchmaking," the company specified.

Azimut shares rose 1.5% to EUR32.84 per share.

By Chiara Bruschi, Alliance News reporter

Comments and questions to redazione@alliancenews.com

Copyright 2026 Alliance News IS Italian Service Ltd. All rights reserved.

data.title: Temu-owner PDD misses revenue estimates
data.description:
March 25 (Reuters) - Discount e-commerce player PDD Holdings missed market estimates for quarterly revenue on Wednesday, as subdued consumer spending weighed on its Chinese platform, Pinduoduo, overshadowing robust growth at its overseas marketplace Temu.

The company reported revenue of 123.9 billion yuan ($17.96 billion) for the fourth quarter, compared with analysts' average estimate of 124.4 billion yuan, according to LSEG data.

($1 = 6.8988 Chinese yuan renminbi)

(Reporting by Kritika Lamba in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

data.title: Sony, Honda Put Brakes on Electric Vehicle JV
data.description:
By Fabiana Negrin Ochoa


Sony Group and Honda Motor are pumping the brakes on a venture that had aimed to make high-end electric vehicles.

Sony Honda Mobility--a joint venture between the two Japanese companies--has decided to discontinue the development and launch of its first model, the Afeela 1, as well as a second vehicle that had been under development.

The program was established during 2022 at the peak of EV fever, and Sony's then-CEO, Kenichiro Yoshida, had expressed high hopes for the venture, expecting the car market to increasingly shift to a service model where customers pay regularly for software downloads and entertainment.

On Wednesday, SHM said that since its inception, it has aimed to make high-value-added mobility products that combine the technologies and expertise of Sony and Honda.

However, due to Honda's reassessment of its electrification strategy, SHM won't be able to use certain technologies and assets originally planned to be provided by the carmaker, and no longer has a viable path to bring the vehicles to market, it said.

Earlier this month, Honda said it expects to swing to its first annual loss in decades due to $15.7 billion in expenses and losses related to the reassessment of its EV strategy. The automaker said it decided to cancel the launches and development of certain models in response to a slowdown in the North American EV market.

Honda said Wednesday that it doesn't expect the joint venture's halt to have a material impact on its forecasts for the fiscal year ending in March.

Meanwhile, Sony said it is evaluating the impact of the discontinuation on its operations and financial position for the fiscal years ending March 2026 and 2027. But as SHM has been operating under a asset-light approach, Sony said it believes the impact won't be significant.

The move comes shortly after Sony Honda Mobility of America announced the grand opening of the Afeela studio and delivery hub in California. SHM said it will issue refunds of reservation fees for Afeela 1 vehicles in California.

The three companies will continue to discuss SHM's future business direction and provide updates when available.


Write to Fabiana Negrin Ochoa at fabiana.negrinochoa@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-25-26 0632ET

Airbus shares climb following major China Eastern Airlines order
data.description:
China Eastern Airlines has placed an order for 101 A320neo aircraft from Airbus, according to Reuters. The European planemaker stated it was "delighted" with the deal.

According to a regulatory filing submitted by the Chinese carrier to the Shanghai Stock Exchange, the total list price for the acquisition amounts to approximately 15.8 billion dollars (13.5 billion euros).

Airbus shares are up 1.6% in Paris.

data.title: Bipartisan group of senators reached deal on capping price of insulin at $35
China Eastern Airlines to Buy Airbus Jets Worth $15.8 Billion
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao


China Eastern Airlines agreed to buy 101 Airbus A320neo jets in a deal worth about $15.8 billion.

The Chinese airline said in a stock exchange filling on Wednesday that the purchase has a total list price of about 108.89 billion yuan, equivalent to $15.80 billion. The jets are scheduled to be delivered in batches from 2028 to 2032, China Eastern said.

The airline said the purchase is part of the company's strategy to plan and reserve fleets in advance, and is pending the Chinese government's approval.

Airbus has granted price concessions on the purchase, China Eastern said, adding that the actual purchase price will be lower than the list price.

The airline said the transaction will be paid in installments and isn't expected to significantly affect the company's cash flow and operations.


Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

03-25-26 0621ET

data.title: PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk Reports Earnings Results for the Full Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
PT Dian Swastatika Sentosa Tbk reported earnings results for the full year ended December 31, 2025. For the full year, the company reported sales was USD 2,791.22 million compared to USD 3,017.8 million a year ago. Net income was USD 230.54 million compared to USD 309.08 million a year ago.

Basic earnings per share from continuing operations was USD 0.0014 compared to USD 0.002 a year ago.
